title: Navigation
objectives: Use declarative routing (go_router) and deep links
Pass and return data between routes

sections.markdown: Navigation moves between screens (routes). Flutter offers the imperative Navigator (a stack of routes) and declarative routers (go_router) that map URLs/state to screens.
Navigator 2.0 made routing declarative so the back stack can be derived from app state (essential for web URLs and deep links). go_router is the recommended wrapper over its complexity.
Use a typed router (go_router) with named routes and path/query params; avoid passing whole objects through routes — pass IDs and refetch, so deep links and restoration work.

flashcards.back: Navigator push/pop a stack; declarative routers map URL/state to screens (go_router).
To make the back stack a function of app state — needed for web URLs and deep linking.

flashcards.front: How return data from a route?
flashcards.back: await Navigator.push(...) returns the value passed to Navigator.pop(result).

quiz.explain: It maps URLs/state to screens declaratively, with deep-link support.

quiz.q: To return a result from a pushed route…
quiz.explain: push returns a Future that completes with pop's value.
Declarative routing supports URLs and deep links.

challenges.text: Push a detail screen and pass an argument.
Return a selected value from a picker screen.
Set up go_router with nested routes and path params.
Implement deep links + auth redirect guards with go_router.

interviewQuestions: How do you pass and return data between routes?
How do deep links work in Flutter?
How do you guard routes (auth redirects)?
